official_sources.title: Statement Execution API: Run SQL on warehouses | Databricks on AWS
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/2.0/sql/statements plus poll GET /{id}, chunk GET /{id}/result/chunks/{index}, cancel POST /{id}/cancel; request fields statement/warehouse_id/catalog/schema/parameters{name,value,type}/wait_timeout (5s-50s, default 10s, 0s async)/on_wait_timeout CONTINUE|CANCEL/disposition INLINE (25 MiB cap) vs EXTERNAL_LINKS (100 GiB cap)/format JSON_ARRAY|ARROW_STREAM|CSV/row_limit/byte_limit; manifest schema.columns {name,type_name,type_text,position}, total_chunk_count/total_row_count/truncated, chunks {chunk_index,row_offset,row_count}; result data_array vs external_links {chunk_index, external_link, expiration, next_chunk_internal_link, next_chunk_index}; Bearer auth; the explicit warning to OMIT the Authorization header when downloading presigned external links

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java StatementStatus.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: status envelope fields: state enum PENDING/RUNNING/SUCCEEDED/FAILED/CANCELED/CLOSED, error (ServiceError), sql_state (SQLSTATE code on failure)

research.language_rationale: The warehouse tooling this exporter joins is written in Go. Databricks documents the Statement Execution API as a plain REST/JSON surface and its official Go SDK is a thin generated wrapper over these same endpoints, so stdlib net/http with encoding/json against the documented paths is the credible zero-dependency integration surface; JSON_ARRAY string-encoded values map naturally onto Go's *string for NULL-preserving typed values.

verified_facts: Statement execution is POST /api/2.0/sql/statements with {statement, warehouse_id, parameters[{name,value,type}], wait_timeout, on_wait_timeout, disposition, format, catalog, schema, row_limit}.
wait_timeout accepts 0s (fully async) or 5s-50s (default 10s); on_wait_timeout is CONTINUE or CANCEL and controls what happens server-side when the wait elapses.
Status polling is GET /api/2.0/sql/statements/{statement_id}; states are PENDING, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, CLOSED; failures carry status.error {error_code, message} and status.sql_state.
The manifest pins schema (columns with name/type_name/type_text/position), total_chunk_count, total_row_count, truncated, and per-chunk {chunk_index, row_offset, row_count}.

disposition INLINE returns string-encoded values in result.data_array with SQL NULL as JSON null (25 MiB cap); EXTERNAL_LINKS returns presigned external_link URLs with expiration plus next_chunk_internal_link/next_chunk_index for traversal (100 GiB cap).

Internal chunk links are relative API paths (/api/2.0/sql/statements/{id}/result/chunks/{index}) requiring Bearer auth; presigned external links embed their own credentials and MUST be fetched without the Authorization header.
API-level failures use the standard Databricks {error_code, message} envelope.
